calculated_metrics.confidence_note: IES is a Back-of-the-Envelope Calculation (BOTEC) to standardize impact across interventions and species. It relies on philosophical and empirical assumptions.

calculated_metrics.name: Impact Equivalency Score (IES)

details.criteria: Pass: <= 2 years of expenditure. | Warn: 2-5 years. | Fail: > 5 years.
Pass: >= 6 months of operating expenses. | Warn: 3-6 months. | Fail: < 3 months.
Pass: At least one intervention is classified as Tier 1 (Systemic Change) or Tier 2 (Prevention & Scalable Care). | Warn: All verifiable interventions are Tier 3 (Direct Care & Indirect Action).

details.formula: Mapping of verified intervention types to the Intervention Leverage Tier framework.

details.calculation: A quantified counterfactual baseline was provided.
details.criteria: Pass: At least one metric has a quantified counterfactual baseline. | Warn: A qualitative baseline is provided, but lacks quantified data. | Fail: No valid counterfactual baseline is provided.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered, helping to control the stray population and prevent countless unwanted litters.'
details.formula: Presence of a quantified counterfactual baseline with a source quote.

details.calculation: Financials with income breakdown are missing.
details.criteria: Pass: Government grants are < 80% of total income. | Fail: Government grants are >= 80% of total income.

details.calculation: Operates in a low-neglectedness / saturated area (Companion Animals: 100%).
details.criteria: Pass: >= 50% of beneficiaries are high-neglectedness (farmed/wild). | Warn: 1-49% are high-neglectedness. | Fail: 0% are high-neglectedness.
details.formula: Proportional analysis of beneficiary populations (farmed/wild vs. companion)

details.calculation: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts.
details.criteria: Bonus: Organisation self-reported on unintended negative impacts. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found.
details.elaboration: Quote: 'TNRM, short for Trap-Neuter-Release-Manage, is an ideaology that while humane is not practical in its implementation, the M stands for managing the strays that would have been release but the strays would still be left to fend for themselves as they would likely be injured through fights and human abuse and natural disasters such as flooding. Therefore, releasing them is not a practical solution.'
details.formula: Check for self-reported unintended consequences or failures.

details.calculation: No disclosure of euthanasia or live-release rates found.
details.criteria: Bonus: Euthanasia/live-release data is disclosed. | Not Disclosed: No disclosure found. | N/A: Not applicable for non-sheltering organisations.
details.elaboration: This check rewards transparency for a sensitive operational metric.
details.formula: For direct rescue orgs, check for euthanasia/live-release rate disclosure.

source.quote: Additionally, through dedicated fundraising efforts, an impressive RM363,000 was raised to further support the Stray Free Selangor (SFS) program, strengthening ongoing spay-and-neuter initiatives.
Over the years, there has been meaningful progress in advancing animal welfare, from lobbying the Animal Welfare Act 2015 to raising awareness and mobilizing resources for impactful initiatives.
In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered, helping to control the stray population and prevent countless unwanted litters.
In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered
In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered, helping to control the stray population and prevent countless unwanted litters.
In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered, helping to control the stray population and prevent countless unwanted litters.
In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered, helping to control the stray population and prevent countless unwanted litters.
In 2024 alone, 4,340 cats and dogs have been neutered, helping to control the stray population and prevent countless unwanted litters.

Since 2003, over 60,000 cats and dogs have been spayed or neutered through our programs across the Klang Valley and beyond. Unlike costly and ineffective measures like dog catching and euthanasia, neutering is a long-term solution that tackles overpopulation at its core.
Since 2003, over 60,000 cats and dogs have been spayed or neutered through our programs across the Klang Valley and beyond.
Meanwhile, 187 dogs, puppies, cats, and kittens found loving homes through adoption.
Meanwhile, 187 dogs, puppies, cats, and kittens found loving homes through adoption.
Since the campaign's launch in March 2017, a total of 14,976 animals have been neutered under the SFS initiative as of December 2024
Since the campaign's launch in March 2017, a total of 14,976 animals have been neutered under the SFS initiative as of December 2024

source.quote: Additionally, through dedicated fundraising efforts, an impressive RM363,000 was raised to further support the Stray Free Selangor (SFS) program, strengthening ongoing spay-and-neuter initiatives.
Additionally, through dedicated fundraising efforts, an impressive RM363,000 was raised to further support the Stray Free Selangor (SFS) program, strengthening ongoing spay-and-neuter initiatives.
In just a few months, the store successfully raised RM13,000, helping SPCA Selangor provide care and resources for animals in need.
In just a few months, the store successfully raised RM13,000, helping SPCA Selangor provide care and resources for animals in need.
SPCA SELANGOR | LIMA RIBU RINGGIT MALAYSIA SAHAJA RM 5000.00
SPCA SELANGOR | LIMA RIBU RINGGIT MALAYSIA SAHAJA RM 5000.00

TNRM, short for Trap-Neuter-Release-Manage, is an ideaology that while humane is not practical in its implementation, the M stands for managing the strays that would have been release but the strays would still be left to fend for themselves as they would likely be injured through fights and human abuse and natural disasters such as flooding. Therefore, releasing them is not a practical solution.
